headline: Alphabet DoD AI Deal Sparks Employee Protests And Investor Questions
summary: Google has revised its contract with the U.S. Department of Defense to provide advanced AI capabilities for classified projects. The updated agreement gives the Pentagon broader access to Alphabet's AI models for secret government work. The move has triggered internal protests, with hundreds of employees urging CEO Sundar Pichai to restrict military use of Google AI tools. For investors watching NasdaqGS:GOOGL, this development sits against a backdrop of strong recent share performance. The...

headline: Market Rally Rides OpenAI Shock Ahead of Fed Decision, Big Tech Earnings. Stock Futures Rise.
summary: Stock futures were rising to kick off a massive day for the market, with a Federal Reserve policy decision and a slew of Big Tech earnings likely to decide if the April rally has more room to run.  The three major indexes all fell on Tuesday after The Wall Street Journal reported that ChatGPT developer OpenAI had missed revenue and user targets, fueling worries about artificial intelligence spending.  Investors appeared to be buying the dip on Wednesday, with Google parent Alphabet, online retailer Amazon, Facebook owner Meta Platforms, and IT giant Microsoft set to report after the closing bell.

content.title: Market Rally Rides OpenAI Shock Ahead of Fed Decision, Big Tech Earnings. Stock Futures Rise.
content.summary: Stock futures were rising to kick off a massive day for the market, with a Federal Reserve policy decision and a slew of Big Tech earnings likely to decide if the April rally has more room to run.  The three major indexes all fell on Tuesday after The Wall Street Journal reported that ChatGPT developer OpenAI had missed revenue and user targets, fueling worries about artificial intelligence spending.  Investors appeared to be buying the dip on Wednesday, with Google parent Alphabet, online retailer Amazon, Facebook owner Meta Platforms, and IT giant Microsoft set to report after the closing bell.

headline: Hyperscaler results pose major test for AI-driven US stock market
summary: A pivotal moment for the artificial intelligence trade driving the U.S. stock market to all-time highs arrives on Wednesday with quarterly reports from four massive companies at the heart of the investment boom behind the new technology.  Results are due after ‌the market closes from Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon and Meta Platforms -- four "hyperscalers" expected to spend over $600 billion this year on data centers and other AI-related infrastructure. ‌Those heavyweight companies represent more than $10 trillion in market capitalization and 17% of the S&P 500's weighting, while their recent gains have helped lead the market's rebound over the past month as stocks have ​shaken off concerns over the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran.  "From a market perspective, they still are the straw that stirs the drinks on big index funds," said Chuck Carlson, chief executive officer at Horizon Investment Services.

content.title: Hyperscaler results pose major test for AI-driven US stock market
content.summary: A pivotal moment for the artificial intelligence trade driving the U.S. stock market to all-time highs arrives on Wednesday with quarterly reports from four massive companies at the heart of the investment boom behind the new technology.  Results are due after ‌the market closes from Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon and Meta Platforms -- four "hyperscalers" expected to spend over $600 billion this year on data centers and other AI-related infrastructure. ‌Those heavyweight companies represent more than $10 trillion in market capitalization and 17% of the S&P 500's weighting, while their recent gains have helped lead the market's rebound over the past month as stocks have ​shaken off concerns over the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran.  "From a market perspective, they still are the straw that stirs the drinks on big index funds," said Chuck Carlson, chief executive officer at Horizon Investment Services.

headline: Fed Meeting: Key Questions As Powell Passes Torch To Warsh
summary: Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell is now presiding over his final rate-setting meeting, followed by a 2 p.m. ET policy statement and 2:30 p.m. news conference.  The biggest question Powell will face, as far as markets are concerned, is whether he will serve out his term as a Fed governor that runs through February 2028.  The Fed's current makeup will make it challenging for Kevin Warsh, Trump's nominee to take over the Fed's helm, to ease monetary policy starting in June.

content.title: Fed Meeting: Key Questions As Powell Passes Torch To Warsh
content.summary: Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell is now presiding over his final rate-setting meeting, followed by a 2 p.m. ET policy statement and 2:30 p.m. news conference.  The biggest question Powell will face, as far as markets are concerned, is whether he will serve out his term as a Fed governor that runs through February 2028.  The Fed's current makeup will make it challenging for Kevin Warsh, Trump's nominee to take over the Fed's helm, to ease monetary policy starting in June.

headline: Team Cymru Launches Pure Signal™ MCP Server, Bringing Agentic AI to the World’s Largest Threat Intelligence Data Ocean
summary: LAKE MARY, Fla., April 29, 2026--Team Cymru today announced the general availability of the Pure Signal™ MCP Server, the first purpose-built, production-grade Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for threat intelligence. Available immediately, the server connects any MCP-compatible AI agent — including Claude, Microsoft Security Copilot, Copilot Studio, GitHub Copilot and custom agents — directly to Team Cymru’s Pure Signal platform, the world’s largest threat intelligence data ocean.

summary: Bill Ackman’s new closed-end investment fund could start trading Wednesday on the New York Stock Exchage at a discount from its offering price of $50 a share.  The size of the offering is disappointing, considering that Ackman had lined up about $2.7 billion of demand from a group of institutional investors before marketing the deal broadly to retail and institutional buyers.

headline: Alphabet investors push for safeguards on use of its cloud, AI tech
summary: A group of Alphabet shareholders are pressing the company to explain how it governs and controls the use of its technology and cloud services by governments for surveillance after the tech ‌giant rejected calls for greater disclosure.  In a letter to Alphabet, seen by Reuters, the group asked for a meeting ‌with management after the Google owner opposed a shareholder resolution seeking a report on how it oversees the related risks.  "Cloud-based services are a growing segment, and it's ​getting more and more militarized," said Marcela Pinilla, director of sustainable investing at Zevin Asset Management, which wrote the letter signed by 42 organizations and 14 individuals managing a combined $1.15 trillion in assets.

content.title: Alphabet investors push for safeguards on use of its cloud, AI tech
content.summary: A group of Alphabet shareholders are pressing the company to explain how it governs and controls the use of its technology and cloud services by governments for surveillance after the tech ‌giant rejected calls for greater disclosure.  In a letter to Alphabet, seen by Reuters, the group asked for a meeting ‌with management after the Google owner opposed a shareholder resolution seeking a report on how it oversees the related risks.  "Cloud-based services are a growing segment, and it's ​getting more and more militarized," said Marcela Pinilla, director of sustainable investing at Zevin Asset Management, which wrote the letter signed by 42 organizations and 14 individuals managing a combined $1.15 trillion in assets.

headline: Fed Meeting: Key Questions As Powell Passes Torch To Warsh (Live Coverage)
summary: Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell is now presiding over his final rate-setting meeting, followed by a 2 p.m. ET policy statement and 2:30 p.m. news conference.  The biggest question Powell will face, as far as markets are concerned, is whether he will serve out his term as a Fed governor that runs through February 2028.  The Fed's current makeup will make it challenging for Kevin Warsh,  Trump's nominee to take over the Fed's helm, to ease monetary policy starting in June.

The biggest question Powell will face, as far as markets are concerned, is whether he will serve out his term as a Fed governor that runs through February 2028.  The Fed's current makeup will make it challenging for Kevin Warsh,  Trump's nominee to take over the Fed's helm, to ease monetary policy starting in June.

headline: Oil prices surge as Trump rejects Iran offer, Disney to keep ESPN

Stay up to date on the latest market action, minute-by-minute, with Yahoo Finance's Market Minute.

headline: Fed Meeting: Powell Passes Torch With Rate Cuts On Ice (Live Coverage)
summary: The Federal Reserve held its key interest rate steady on Wednesday as Chairman Jerome Powell presided over his final meeting.  Kevin Warsh, President Donald Trump's nominee to replace him, will take over prior to the next meeting on June 17.  With oil prices continuing to jump on Thursday, markets see negligible odds of a rate cut before the end of the year.

content.title: Fed Meeting: Powell Passes Torch With Rate Cuts On Ice (Live Coverage)
content.summary: The Federal Reserve held its key interest rate steady on Wednesday as Chairman Jerome Powell presided over his final meeting.  Kevin Warsh, President Donald Trump's nominee to replace him, will take over prior to the next meeting on June 17.  With oil prices continuing to jump on Thursday, markets see negligible odds of a rate cut before the end of the year.

headline: Yes, There Is An Alternative To Google And Bing. Meet Opera.
summary: Among the alternatives to Alphabet's Google web browser and Microsoft's Edge and Bing is the Opera browser.  On Tuesday Opera reported strong earnings and its stock popped 5.3%.  On Wednesday, the IBD Relative Strength (RS) Rating for Opera stock, which trades on the Nasdaq index as OPRA, shot up 10 points, from 70 to 80.

content.title: Yes, There Is An Alternative To Google And Bing. Meet Opera.
content.summary: Among the alternatives to Alphabet's Google web browser and Microsoft's Edge and Bing is the Opera browser.  On Tuesday Opera reported strong earnings and its stock popped 5.3%.  On Wednesday, the IBD Relative Strength (RS) Rating for Opera stock, which trades on the Nasdaq index as OPRA, shot up 10 points, from 70 to 80.
